purpose: Durable, LLM-readable Codex collaboration history for the ACS project. Update this file after meaningful planning, architecture decisions, milestone work, restores, or user corrections so future assistants do not lose project history.
updatePolicy: Append a new entry after each milestone or major conversation decision.
Summarize user intent, implemented changes, explicit decisions, unresolved questions, and follow-up obligations.
Do not store secrets or credentials.
Keep entries concise but specific enough for an LLM to reconstruct project direction.
standingUserPreferences: Explain in detail what was done after completed work.
Commit and push completed milestones to GitHub.
Update User Guide, System Reference, and AI-friendly docs after each milestone.
Keep editor UI progressively disclosed: only show controls relevant to the active task.
Use creative tutorials that showcase triggers, entities, tiles, items, quests, and action chains rather than trivial label edits.
Preserve architecture flexibility for HD 2D, 3D, robust audio, realtime mode, AI-assisted authoring, and AI-driven NPCs.
Treat reusable game concepts as objects with stable ids and CRUD rather than loose strings.
Use cyclomatic complexity greater than 8 as a refactor trigger and avoid new SOLID violations.

priorConversationHistory.summary: This section reconstructs the pre-log Codex conversation from thread context. It is intentionally structured as a decision and milestone digest rather than a verbatim transcript so future LLMs can recover project intent quickly.
projectOrigin: The user wants a modern remake/reimagining of Adventure Construction Set inspired by the 1984 application, beginning with an 8-bit/classic experience but architected for future high-resolution 2D, 3D, richer audio, realtime play, and AI-assisted creation.
The initial technical direction emphasized separation between engine rules, content schema, renderer/presentation, persistence, editor-core operations, and web UI.
The user repeatedly stressed that early architectural choices must keep future phases flexible rather than locking the project to one retro implementation.
earlyDesignQuestions: The user asked what manifest-based asset references mean. Decision: game data should point to stable asset ids and manifests rather than hardcoded file names or assumptions, so visual/audio styles can be swapped later.

The user asked about enemy AI, editor behavior, computer-finished adventures, and describing a game to the application. Planning response: start with deterministic behavior profiles and structured editor data, later add AI-assisted authoring and possibly autonomous adventure completion through validated object proposals.
The user requested phase plans and an operational roadmap before implementation began.
repoAndProcess: Repo path established as H:/My Drive/Repos/ACS, GitHub remote https://github.com/ianohlander/ACS.git.
The user asked that each milestone be committed and pushed to GitHub when complete.
The user later clarified that Codex should not ask permission for routine inspection, documentation regeneration, or repo modifications, only when the tool requires elevated execution.
The user asked for detailed post-step explanations after completed work.

completedMilestoneDigest.notes: Created the monorepo foundation, documentation scaffolding, architecture boundaries, and ADRs.
Created shared package types and raw content normalization concepts.
Added renderer-agnostic game session/state concepts, commands, movement, interactions, dialogue, triggers, and snapshots.
Added a playable web runtime, sample adventure, canvas rendering, map movement, Oracle interaction, shrine reward, and map travel.
Added save/load/reset using runtime snapshots so persistence wraps the existing runtime state model rather than inventing a second state model.

Added basic enemy behavior profiles and turn pacing. User later flagged that the wolf moving with every player move felt wrong; turn interval pacing became important to preserve classic turn-based feel.
Added browser editor and editor-core primitives for metadata, map/tile work, validation, and guide documentation.
Added project API and project/release concepts, then generated PDF and guide documentation.
Added shared publish readiness checks and began tightening documentation quality.
Added entity placement from reusable definitions, singleton vs multiple placement policy, and validation around singleton placement.

Added a classic ACS-inspired presentation mode with fixed gameplay panel, map viewport, status rail, and bottom message band. User requested larger-than-original scale and keyboard-consistent interactions.

Added manifest-backed classic sprite references. User clarified that visual manifests should not store all future graphics in sampleAdventure and that presentation must remain separate from engine rules.
Added editing of entity definition metadata, placement policy, behavior, faction, sprites, and related fields.
Added editing for dialogue and structured triggers. User emphasized keyboard-first dialogue in classic mode and appropriate dialogue visibility based on debug/classic mode.
Added map categories, map metadata, blank map creation, and editor map organization.
Added profile data, stats, skills, and starting possessions. User later requested a meaningful player profile panel for inventory, skills, abilities, traits, and connected objects.
Added guided When/If/Then controls and actions such as item gates, quest/flag checks, dialogue, rewards, teleporters, and tile changes.
Added trigger create/duplicate/delete and map-cell trigger markers.

Refactored editor into focused areas: Adventure, World Atlas, Map Workspace, Libraries, Logic, and Test & Publish. User liked the left-side edit flow but asked to apply progressive disclosure everywhere.
Added map selectors to Map Workspace and Logic and began classifying libraries into reusable object kinds.
Added editable exits/portal links and map graph summaries. Later clarification: exit is the runtime map-link record; portal is designer-facing fiction/presentation for a travel link.
Added editable tile definitions with passability, hints, tags, and visuals. User emphasized tiles, entities, items, and similar values should all become CRUD-enabled objects.
Added quest and objective editing. User later objected to objectives as loose strings and wanted object-backed, categorized objectives to avoid infinite dropdowns and duplicate pollution.
Converted quest stages/objectives and rewards toward object-backed structures and started broader cleanup toward everything-as-objects.

completedMilestoneDigest.theme: Presentation, pixel art, and starter libraries
completedMilestoneDigest.notes: Added adventure splash/music/intro settings, classic 8x8 pixel sprite data, an Assets panel with pixel editing, and initial genre starter packs. User later found starter packs too sparse and asked to incorporate original ACS starter-pack data, built-in pixel editing for all visual objects, and genre selection when creating a new adventure.
Added editor-core diagnostics, Test & Publish diagnostics, and npm run playtest:smoke. Last stable pushed commit before current work: d67ed01 Complete milestone 25 diagnostics harness.

importantCorrectionsAndPreferences: The user disliked helper functions living beside raw sampleAdventure content. Decision: game content should be raw data; helper functions should belong in loader/normalization modules.
The user disliked the map viewport moving down the screen to keep the player centered. Classic presentation should keep the play panel visually stable.
The user requested documentation as PDFs with screenshots and diagrams, and later repeatedly corrected broken/overlapped screenshots and diagrams.
The user requested the User Guide tutorial be creative and rich, especially once triggers, tiles, entities, items, and starter libraries mature.
The user asked the System Reference to be exhaustive, with architecture, sequence, Mermaid, screenshots, and end-to-end behavior explanations.
The user asked for AI-friendly documentation containing application structure, data, and flow for LLM consumption.
The user requested that functions be organized across all code, not only editor-core, with related interfaces/functions grouped and collapsible section comments where appropriate.
The user requested automated complexity checks with max cyclomatic complexity 8 and gradual cleanup of legacy violations.
editorInformationArchitectureDecisions: The editor should not show everything at once. It should follow a hierarchical game-design flow.
Libraries should drill down left-to-right/top-to-bottom: choose library type, choose category/class, choose object, then edit details full-width below.
Detail panels should group fields thematically rather than stretching all inputs across the full width.
Graphics editing should be available in the detail panel for any object with a visual presentation, not only entity definitions or the Assets focus.

Map Workspace should show task-specific controls only: tile controls while painting tiles, entity controls while placing/moving entities, exit/portal controls while editing travel links, and trigger controls while editing triggers.
Logic and Map Workspace should both allow switching maps directly without returning to World Atlas.
libraryAndObjectModelDecisions: Entities, items, tiles, quests, dialogue, flags, spells, traits, skills, rewards, objectives, categories, media cues, trigger templates, and condition/action templates should become stable-id objects with CRUD.
Values should not exist only as comma-separated text lists or unorganized dropdown values.
Objectives need organization by genre/type/purpose to avoid infinite dropdowns, near duplicates, and data pollution.
Duplicate and near-duplicate warnings should compare normalized names, aliases, descriptions, tags, categories, and target references before object creation.
Starter packs should be curated genre collections over shared library objects, not separate engines or hardcoded rulesets.

Default starter libraries should move out of sampleAdventure into reusable package/domain/library data. Individual adventures can include custom additions and overrides. Designers should be able to import/export custom library packs.
classicAcsAndLegacyResearch: The user provided legacy ACS screenshots and asked how to evoke the original without recreating the clunky UI exactly.
Classic ACS visual mode should capture the feel: gameplay panel, symbolic tiles, status rail, bottom message band, vintage palette, and pixel-art editing.
The user provided legacy images and a file named legacy images/legacy_ACS_startpacks.txt listing original ACS-style categories and examples.
The user asked to track legacy_ACS_startpacks.txt in git and use it to expand starter libraries toward the original ACS set.
aiPlanningDecisions: Adventure generation should be AI-provider agnostic through an adapter/interface. Different LLMs or agents should be usable if they expose an API.
AI-generated content must enter as structured proposals, then pass schema validation, duplicate warnings, provenance, and human review before mutating a draft.
Optional AI-driven NPCs are a later phase. They should call the same validated action services as the player, not private shortcuts.
Designers should provide NPC brain records: history, backstory, motives, relationships, knowledge boundaries, voice, memory policy, and story-role constraints.

NPC capabilities must be explicit. Some NPCs may be support, informational, random, rivals, or antagonists. Designers decide which items, triggers, portals, maps, and action categories each NPC may use. Individual objects may still be player-only, NPC-only, blocked, all-actor, or allow-listed.
currentOpenWorkFromUser: Continue from the stable milestone 25 baseline and avoid corrupted half-applied changes.
Keep docs/codex-chat-log.json updated with durable project history.
Answer and document the portal vs exit distinction.
Plan stacked tile/action effects for splash/media, stat changes, item grant/removal, NPC spawn/despawn, tile changes, quest updates, sound, and travel.
Ensure all reusable gameplay concepts become CRUD-enabled objects.
Plan and eventually implement a player profile panel.
Move default starter libraries outside sampleAdventure and support import/export custom libraries.
Apply progressive-disclosure editor UI to Map Workspace and other editor areas.
Track and analyze legacy_ACS_startpacks.txt and use it to create richer starter libraries.
Replace the User Guide tutorial with a new Land-of-Adventuria-style quest-building walkthrough with copious screenshots.

entries.summary: Created the durable Codex chat log after the user requested that chat history be kept in an LLM-readable file. Prior work through Milestone 25 included architecture docs, runtime/editor scaffolding, persistence, enemy turns, classic ACS visual mode, editor library organization, quest objects, pixel-art/presentation/starter libraries, diagnostics, and a playtest smoke harness.
currentUserRequests: Plan stacked tile effects with splash/media, boosts, item grant/removal, and NPC spawn.
Add a meaningful player profile panel for state, inventory, abilities, and connected objects.
Move starter libraries out of sampleAdventure into reusable default/custom library storage over time.
Ensure Map Workspace and other editor UIs show only controls relevant to the selected task.
Track legacy_ACS_startpacks.txt and use it to expand starter libraries.
Replace the User Guide tutorial with a fresh Land-of-Adventuria-style quest creation walkthrough.

entries.summary: After an interrupted starter-library expansion, restored the malformed sampleAdventure edit, repaired committed comma separators in sampleAdventure, verified the TypeScript parser and npm quality gate, and updated roadmap/LLM context planning for stacked tile effects, universal object CRUD, player profile panel, reusable starter-library storage, and progressive editor disclosure.
decisions: An exit is the runtime map-link record; a portal is the designer-facing/story label that may use the same exit traversal machinery.
Default starter libraries should migrate out of sampleAdventure into reusable library data while individual games keep custom additions and overrides.
Legacy ACS starter-pack source data should be tracked in git and used as the input for expanding curated classic starter libraries.
verification: npm run quality passed with no new or worsened complexity violations.

entries.summary: Expanded the new User Guide tutorial with additional screenshots and diagrams so the Land-of-Adventuria-style quest walkthrough is more visual. Verified that all referenced guide image assets exist.

Deleted the fantasy Moon Key tutorial and replaced it with a science-fiction tutorial called Relay Station Alecto. The new tutorial demonstrates the most complex current trigger chain supported by the application: dialogue, flags, quest stages, item grants, tile changes, teleport actions, normal exits, diagnostics, and playtesting. Regenerated user-guide.html, user-guide.pdf, and system-reference.pdf, and patched the System Reference HTML/Markdown tutorial requirement to match the science-fiction tutorial.
verification: No Moon Key or Beneath The Oracle references remain in user-guide.md or user-guide.html.
User Guide HTML has 38 image references and 0 missing image assets.
System Reference HTML has 8 image references and 0 missing image assets.

entries.summary: Replaced generic tutorial images with 16 generated Relay Station Alecto step screenshots that match the science-fiction tutorial instructions. The tutorial now shows purpose-built visuals for adventure setup, world atlas planning, map painting, tile concepts, item/assets selection, cast placement, quest stages, dialogue states, each major trigger chain, diagnostics, and final playtest flow.
verification: User Guide tutorial contains 34 image references and all 34 are tutorial-relay assets.
No generic editor/runtime screenshots remain inside the tutorial section.

entries.summary: Started and completed the first Milestone 26 library portability foundation. Added packages/default-content as @acs/default-content, defining built-in starter library source metadata, starter snapshot helpers, custom library export records, object collection copying, genre filtering, and object-count summaries. Updated roadmap, User Guide, System Reference, PDFs, LLM context, and app labels to document the starter/custom library storage split.
decisions: Built-in starter libraries are application-owned default content.
Adventure-local objects live in the project/adventure package and may diverge from defaults.
Reusable custom libraries should export as separate designer-owned files with schema version, genre tags, provenance, starter-pack references, and object collections.

The browser sample records starter-library provenance as a string tag for now instead of importing @acs/default-content directly, because npm install hit an existing workspace symlink issue on node_modules/@acs/runtime-2d.
verification: Known-good TypeScript compiler at C:/Codex/tools/tsc-runner built the repo successfully.
npm run quality:complexity passed with no new or worsened complexity violations.
Fixed an exactOptionalPropertyTypes issue in editor-core diagnostics while verifying with the stronger compiler.
deferred: New Adventure UI for selecting starter genres.
Import/export file pickers for custom library files.
Moving all concrete starter objects out of sampleAdventure.ts into default-content data modules.
Resolving the existing npm install workspace symlink issue before making browser runtime depend directly on @acs/default-content.

entries.summary: Incorporated future publishing modes into the roadmap, System Reference, and LLM project context. The project should eventually publish both forkable construction packages and standalone play-only games from immutable releases.
decisions: Forkable exports include editable source, authoring metadata, custom libraries, provenance, visual manifests, assets, and remix/fork licensing metadata.
Standalone playable exports include runtime-only package data, required assets, a play shell, distribution metadata, and exclude editor UI, draft history, source notes, and unused starter content.
Publishing transforms a release into artifacts and must not mutate the original project draft or release snapshot.
A future packages/publishing boundary should own artifact generation, asset dependency collection, pruning, and validation.
Static web bundles should be the first standalone target; desktop wrappers can come later.

entries.summary: Fixed a Map Workspace progressive-disclosure bug where exit/portal target controls could remain visible while Terrain Tiles mode was selected. Replaced repeated Relay Station Alecto tutorial graphics with unique step-specific detail graphics and expanded the tutorial steps for creating region maps and linking the Data Core Chamber back to the Access Ring.
decisions: The hidden utility class now uses display: none !important so later layout rules such as .definition-grid cannot accidentally override selected-mode visibility.
Tutorial graphics should be meaningful to the exact step. Broad screenshots remain useful for orientation, but repeated images should be replaced by focused detail graphics or removed.
The map-linking tutorial now explicitly shows the Exits & Portals workflow and states that target-map controls appear only in that mode.
verification: TypeScript workspace build passed with the known-good compiler at C:/Codex/tools/tsc-runner.
npm run quality:complexity passed with no new or worsened violations over 8.

entries.summary: Added per-instance entity naming and simple behavior overrides so multiple placed copies of the same reusable entity definition can have unique story roles such as Station AI Alecto, Pad Sentry Drone, Red Team Spy Handler, or Green Team Spy Handler. Replaced the User Guide tutorial with a beginner-focused Relay Station Alecto walkthrough driven by real screenshots captured from the editor interface.
decisions: EntityDefinition remains the reusable template, while EntityInstance is the placed copy on a map.
EntityInstance now supports optional displayName and behaviorOverride metadata.
The runtime enemy turn system resolves behavior by checking the placed instance override first and falling back to the reusable definition behavior.
The Map Workspace Entity Instances mode now exposes Instance Name and Behavior Override controls when placing or updating entities.
The tutorial must show actual UI state from the editor instead of vague generated graphics or repeated screenshots.
verification: TypeScript workspace build passed with the known-good compiler at C:/Codex/tools/tsc-runner.
npm run quality:complexity passed with no new or worsened violations over 8 after refactoring applyEntityEdit.
Generated tutorial-ui-01-editor-open.png through tutorial-ui-16-ready-to-playtest.png from the actual editor interface.
Updated User Guide Markdown/HTML and System Reference Markdown/HTML to explain instance naming, behavior overrides, and the real UI tutorial flow.

entries.summary: Completed Milestone 27 editor information architecture slice. Added a selected-cell inspector in Map Workspace and a UI-based Display Rename / Reskin tool in Test & Publish. The rename feature previews and applies player-facing text changes while preserving stable ids and structured references.
decisions: Selected-cell inspection is derived from the active AdventurePackage draft rather than a separate state model.
Display rename lives in editor-core so preview/apply behavior can later be reused by API workflows, undo/redo, validation, import/export, and publishing.
Display rename is intentionally separate from internal id refactoring. It changes names, descriptions, dialogue text, quest text, and similar display fields, not ids or references.
The Relay Station Alecto tutorial now includes real UI screenshots for selected-cell inspection and display rename preview.
verification: npm run quality:complexity passed with no new or worsened violations over 8.
Captured tutorial-ui-17-selected-cell-inspector.png and tutorial-ui-18-display-rename-preview.png from the actual editor interface.

entries.summary: Accepted and documented future planning for multiplayer and mobile play. Multiplayer should be planned around backend-authoritative sessions with portable runtime-core validation. Mobile support should focus on play-only phone experiences, not mobile editing or creation.
decisions: Phone-sized devices should hide or block editor/create workflows through screen-size and capability gates.
A future mobile shell should provide touch controls, readable scaling, message-band support, and a profile/inventory drawer while dispatching the same runtime commands as desktop play.
Multiplayer should use serializable actor commands sent to an authoritative backend session that validates through runtime-core and broadcasts snapshots/events.
Peer-to-peer can be explored later as an optional private mode, but it should not be the initial multiplayer foundation.
All accepted future recommendations should be mirrored into durable pre-milestone documents so the project memory does not depend on chat alone.

entries.summary: Completed Milestone 28 classic presentation and media/sound cue slice. Added renderer-local Classic Size scaling, first-class mediaCues and soundCues collections, playMedia/playSound trigger actions, cue validation, cue runtime events, editor action-builder controls, sample-adventure cue chains, and documentation updates.
decisions: Cue definitions are adventure data objects that point at assets; runtime-core emits typed cue events instead of hardcoding browser audio or video behavior.
Classic Size is a local presentation preference, not saved adventure state. It changes renderer metrics only.
No-code trigger authoring now includes Play Media Cue and Play Sound Cue actions.
New cue handling uses table-driven summaries/descriptors to avoid growing complex switch functions.
The User Guide tutorial remains Relay Station Alecto and should push the app to its current limits with chained actions, media cues, sound cues, exits, quests, diagnostics, and playtesting.
verification: Built and typechecked with the known-good compiler at C:/Codex/tools/tsc-runner/node_modules/typescript/lib/tsc.js because repo-local node_modules/typescript is corrupt.
Complexity check passed with no new or worsened violations over 8.
Smoke playtest passed and showed mediaCuePlayed/soundCuePlayed events in the Oracle and shrine trigger chains.
Updated docs/roadmap.html, docs/user-guide.md/html, docs/system-reference.md/html, docs/llm-project-context.json, and docs/codex-chat-log.json.
entries.knownIssue: Repo-local node_modules/typescript/package.json and lib/tsc.js are zero-byte files. npm install failed on the Google Drive workspace with an EISDIR workspace symlink error, so normal npm build/typecheck currently no-op until tooling is repaired.

entries.summary: Fixed Map Workspace startup regression where Terrain Tiles mode showed no Tile Brush options. The editor module had been rendering before table-driven action summary constants were initialized, causing a browser ReferenceError and preventing editor startup. Also expanded tile brush population to include all reusable tile definitions and regenerated tutorial screenshots/PDFs.
decisions: Bootstrap is queued after module initialization so const-based summary tables are initialized before renderEditor can summarize trigger actions.
Tile Brush options now come from reusable tile definitions first, then current-map tile palette, then fallback legacy tile ids.
Screenshot capture now waits for map and tile option population and waits after tutorial map creation before capturing images.
verification: Headless Chromium verified map-select has options and tile-select has 27 brush options in Terrain Tiles mode.
Regenerated tutorial screenshots; painting screenshots show populated maps and active brush state.
Regenerated User Guide and System Reference PDFs.
Complexity check passed with no new or worsened violations over 8.

entries.summary: Added a live Grouping Preview to the classic pixel editor so sprite art can be reviewed as repeated neighboring tiles, matching the useful terrain-preview concept from the original ACS graphic editor. Also added a visible paint-color swatch beside the palette dropdown so designers can see the loaded brush color before painting.
decisions: The grouping preview belongs in the Pixel Edit Details pane beside the in-game and magnified previews.
The grouping preview should render to the right of the other previews on wide screens and wrap naturally on narrow screens.
The paint color dropdown should be visual-first. Raw hex codes can remain as tooltips, but the default editing surface should show a swatch and readable color number.
Documentation screenshots for tutorial steps should be meaningful, current, and step-specific rather than repeated generic editor images.
verificationPlanned: Regenerate the Relay Station tutorial screenshots from the live editor.
Regenerate User Guide and System Reference PDFs.
Run TypeScript build, complexity gate, and smoke playtest before commit.

entries.summary: User requested a project pause to add full unit and UI testing before the application grows further. Milestone 29A now establishes a test harness and makes full testing part of every milestone completion.
decisions: Every milestone completion must run npm test, or explicitly document any unavailable test layer before calling the milestone complete.

The first harness stays dependency-light: Node's built-in test runner for package unit tests, PowerShell/CDP headless Chromium tests for the real editor UI and real runtime UI, and the existing command-level runtime smoke playtest.

Coverage output is prepared through NODE_V8_COVERAGE but disabled by default on the current Node 18 Windows runtime because it crashes the process. A later tooling cleanup should stabilize coverage reporting.
Future features and bug fixes should add focused tests at the lowest relevant layer whenever feasible.
implementation: Added tools/run-unit-tests.mjs.
Added tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1.
Added tools/runtime-ui-e2e.ps1.
Added tests/unit for runtime-core, editor-core, validation, and persistence.
Added npm scripts for test:ui:editor, test:ui:runtime, test:ui, test:e2e, and npm test.
Added docs/testing-strategy.md and updated engineering quality, User Guide, System Reference, and LLM project context.
verification: npm test passed: unit tests, headless editor UI smoke, headless runtime UI E2E, and runtime playtest smoke.
npm run test:coverage passed in coverage-capable mode with V8 coverage emission disabled by default on this Node 18 Windows runtime.
Complexity check passed with no new or worsened violations over 8.

entries.summary: Reviewed docs/application QA notes.txt after adding the full testing gate and resolved the listed editor/tutorial clarity issues.
decisions: Simplify the editor-facing map taxonomy to World, Region, and Local Area. Keep broader domain MapKind compatibility, but do not expose confusing interior/dungeon-floor categories in normal editor flow.
Treat Parent Region as organizational metadata only. Exits remain free to connect valid maps inside or across regions.
Use lightweight tooltip/title hints on structural controls such as Map Scale and Parent Region.
Make the Relay Station Alecto tutorial more explicit about object-backed quest objectives and trigger chains instead of relying on vague summary screenshots.
Document current media/sound cue support accurately: media and sound cue objects plus runtime events are supported; real file upload/playback remains later.
implementation: Updated apps/web/editor.html labels, options, and title help for Map Scale and Parent Region.
Changed the built-in shrine sample map to the Local Area scale.
Expanded User Guide Step 12 with five objective objects and reward-object instructions.
Expanded User Guide Step 14 with AI contact, terminal, transit-pad, and data-core trigger recipes plus media/sound cue guidance.
Updated System Reference map hierarchy guidance and application QA notes with resolution status.

entries.summary: Started the next Milestone 29 quality slice after syncing the starter-pack commits to GitHub. The slice hardens standard build/typecheck commands and adds regression coverage for classic ACS starter content.
decisions: Standard npm build and typecheck should use the same resilient compiler-resolution strategy as the test harness instead of directly calling one local TypeScript path.
Default starter-library imports should be protected by tests that compare against the non-Land legacy ACS starter-pack source list.
Land of Aventuria-specific elements should stay out of the generic classic ACS starter-pack import unless explicitly handled as reference/sample-adventure content.
implementation: Added tools/build-workspace.mjs.
Updated npm run build and npm run typecheck to call the resilient build runner.
Updated tools/run-unit-tests.mjs to delegate its build step to tools/build-workspace.mjs.
Added tests/unit/default-content.test.mjs for classic ACS starter-content coverage.
Updated engineering quality notes, System Reference, roadmap, and LLM-readable project context.
verification: npm test passed, including the new default-content unit test.
npm run quality:complexity passed with no new or worsened violations over 8.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 29 with actor-capable runtime test planning. Added the first runtime-side readiness evaluator for future shared player/NPC actions.
decisions: Do not make AI or deterministic NPCs mutate state yet. First add a pure preflight evaluator that can answer whether an actor may attempt an item, trigger, or exit action.
Use existing domain planning types: RuntimeActionProposal, ActorCapabilityProfile, ActorUsePolicy, ActorActionKind, and ActorPermissionMode.
Keep this layer in runtime-core so future browser, diagnostics, deterministic NPC, AI NPC, and multiplayer systems can share one permission interpretation.
implementation: Added packages/runtime-core/src/actor-permissions.ts.
Exported evaluateRuntimeActionReadiness and ActorActionReadiness from runtime-core.
Extended runtime-core unit tests for player item use, enemy item denial, support NPC exit readiness, and informational NPC item-use blocking.
Updated System Reference, roadmap, testing strategy, and LLM-readable project context.
verification: npm run quality:complexity passed with no new or worsened violations over 8.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 29 by adding an executable documentation validation gate for guide/reference screenshots and PDFs.
decisions: Broken guide graphics, missing PDFs, missing tutorial step screenshots, and duplicate tutorial step screenshots should fail a local quality command instead of relying only on manual review.
The validator should check the current Relay Station tutorial Steps 1 through 19 because that tutorial is now the feature acceptance walkthrough.
Human visual review is still required for screenshot quality, but automated validation catches the recurring file/link/repetition failures.
implementation: Added tools/validate-docs.mjs.
Added npm run docs:validate.
Updated npm run quality to run complexity, docs validation, and typecheck.
Updated engineering quality notes, testing strategy, System Reference, roadmap, and LLM-readable project context.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 29 by making the Relay Station Alecto User Guide tutorial an explicit acceptance artifact.
decisions: The tutorial should remain creative and feature-rich, but its required steps, concepts, and screenshots should be machine-checkable.
Documentation validation should read a manifest rather than burying tutorial expectations only inside validator code.
Future milestone features that belong in the tutorial should update both the guide and docs/tutorial-acceptance.json.
implementation: Added docs/tutorial-acceptance.json with the Relay Station Alecto Step 1 through Step 19 tutorial contract.
Updated tools/validate-docs.mjs to validate step titles, required text cues, required screenshots, PDF outputs, image references, and duplicate screenshot reuse.
Updated roadmap, System Reference, testing strategy, engineering quality notes, and LLM-readable project context.
verification: npm run docs:validate passed after manifest alignment.

entries.summary: Started Milestone 30 with the packaging foundation: a new pure publishing package that distinguishes editable forkable exports from standalone play-only exports.
decisions: Begin Milestone 30 with the export artifact boundary before adding browser export UI or static bundle generation.
Publishing must transform cloned adventure or release data into artifacts without mutating the draft or release source of truth.
Standalone runtime dependency collection should only track file-backed AssetRecord ids and cue assets, not manifest-only classic sprite or pixel sprite identifiers.
implementation: Added packages/publishing with createForkableProjectExport, createStandaloneRuntimeExport, collectRuntimeAssets, pruneUnusedAuthoringData, and validatePublishArtifact.
Added workspace TypeScript references and path mapping for @acs/publishing.
Added tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs covering editable forkable exports, standalone artifact stripping, runtime asset collection, and missing runtime asset detection.
Updated roadmap, System Reference, User Guide export notes, version history, and LLM-readable project context.
verification: npm run test:unit passed with the new publishing tests.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by wiring the publishing package into the API, project client, and editor Test and Publish workflow.
decisions: Artifact export should start from immutable releases, not directly from mutable drafts.
The local API should expose a release artifact route rather than having the browser rebuild publishing logic on its own.
The editor should download JSON artifacts now, while later Milestone 30 work handles full distributable bundle packaging.
implementation: Added POST /api/releases/:id/artifacts in apps/api.
Added exportReleaseArtifact(...) to packages/project-api.
Added Export Forkable JSON and Export Standalone JSON controls to the editor Project and Release panel.
Added tests/unit/project-api.test.mjs and extended tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1 for export-control coverage.
Refactored the API route dispatcher and project panel rendering so the complexity gate stayed green.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by turning standalone exports into bundle-aware release artifacts that can describe a real static play shell.
decisions: Keep packages/publishing responsible for artifact identity and validation, but let apps/api own filesystem-aware bundle assembly.
Teach the browser runtime to load a packaged adventure through a URL parameter so standalone bundles use the same runtime code path as normal play mode.
Preserve the no-mutation rule: attaching a bundle must return a new artifact rather than editing draft or release state in place.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing with StandaloneBundleFile and StandaloneBundleManifest plus attachStandaloneBundle and bundle validation rules.
Added apps/api/src/standalone-bundle.ts to assemble a standalone play-shell manifest with index.html, CSS, runtime modules, bundle/adventure-package.json, and standalone metadata.
Updated apps/api/src/index.ts so standalone release exports automatically include the generated bundle manifest.
Updated apps/web/src/index.ts so the runtime can boot from ?package=... and treat a packaged adventure as a standalone source.
Added tests/unit/standalone-bundle.test.mjs and extended publishing tests for attached bundle validation.
Fixed the shared test helper so loadSampleAdventure() returns cloned data instead of leaking mutations between tests.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by turning standalone exports into real ZIP downloads instead of only JSON-with-bundle-manifest artifacts.
decisions: Keep the release API returning typed standalone artifacts rather than adding a separate binary export route immediately.
Put ZIP creation in packages/publishing so archive packaging logic stays shared and testable instead of living only in the browser UI.
Keep forkable export JSON-based while standalone export becomes the first actual distributable package.
implementation: Added packages/publishing/src/standalone-archive.ts with pure TypeScript ZIP archive generation for standalone bundle manifests.
Re-exported createStandaloneBundleArchive from packages/publishing/src/index.ts.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts so standalone exports package the returned bundle manifest into a .zip download.
Renamed the editor action to Export Standalone ZIP and added the missing @acs/publishing browser import-map entries.
Extended standalone bundle unit coverage to verify the generated ZIP contains expected packaged files.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by adding a release-backed standalone package preview to Test and Publish.
decisions: The standalone preview should use the same release-backed standalone artifact source as ZIP export rather than inventing a second packaging path.
Preview state should be invalidated whenever the draft changes so the editor cannot quietly show stale release contents against a modified draft.
Keep the preview compact and task-focused: summary plus shipped file list, not a second full packaging wizard.
implementation: Added Preview Standalone Package button and preview card in apps/web/editor.html.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts to fetch the latest release standalone artifact on demand, cache it as preview state, and render bundle summary lines and packaged file paths.
Cleared latestStandalonePreview whenever markValidationDirty() runs.
Extended tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1 to cover the preview button and preview panel.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by adding a release readiness and known-limitations panel to Test and Publish.
decisions: Designers should not have to mentally combine validation, diagnostics, package preview, and roadmap notes to judge whether a release is ready to share.
The readiness panel should be descriptive rather than a second validator: summarize current state and known MVP limits without replacing validation.
Current MVP limitations should be surfaced in the editor UI, not only in roadmap/reference documents.
implementation: Added a Release Readiness card in apps/web/editor.html.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts to compute readiness from validation, release existence, standalone preview state, diagnostics presence, and current known limitations.
Refactored project action-button state into smaller helpers to satisfy the complexity gate while extending the packaging workflow.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by adding publish-time release notes and label metadata to immutable releases.
decisions: Publish-only notes should live on the immutable release record, not inside the adventure package draft.
The editor should collect release label and notes before publish, then surface that metadata back in release summaries and readiness checks.
Milestone label drift in browser page titles should be fixed while closing out this release-polish slice.
implementation: Extended packages/project-api/src/index.ts so ReleaseRecord and PublishReleaseRequest include releaseNotes.
Updated apps/api/src/index.ts to normalize and persist releaseNotes on published release records.
Added a Release Notes card with release-label and release-notes inputs in apps/web/editor.html.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts so publishRelease() sends the entered metadata, recent release summary items include note excerpts, and the readiness checklist reports release-notes presence.
Updated tests/unit/project-api.test.mjs and tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1 for the new release metadata flow.
Updated apps/web/editor.html and apps/web/index.html titles to Milestone 30.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by adding a first-class distribution manifest to standalone exports and bundles.
decisions: Standalone packaging should carry one canonical distribution summary rather than scattering release identity across bundle files and preview UI text.
The immutable release record should remain the source for packaged release id, label, version, and release notes.
The editor should read packaged distribution metadata back during preview/readiness instead of inventing a second parallel summary model.
implementation: Added StandaloneDistributionManifest to packages/publishing/src/index.ts and included it on standalonePlayable artifacts.
Updated apps/api/src/index.ts so standalone exports pass release id, label, version, and release notes into the publishing layer.
Updated apps/api/src/standalone-bundle.ts to emit bundle/distribution-manifest.json.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts so standalone preview and release readiness surface distribution-manifest data such as release label, manifest file presence, and packaged limitation counts.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and tests/unit/standalone-bundle.test.mjs to verify the new manifest.

entries.summary: Reviewed the recent project-memory trail and updated the roadmap to capture standalone delivery-mode planning explicitly.
decisions: Standalone export should remain one portable static web bundle even as distribution options multiply.
Planned delivery options should explicitly include manual hosting, hosted web sharing, a bundled local launcher with an embedded tiny web server, and later desktop wrappers.
The last few days of durable project memory did not reveal additional uncaptured roadmap requirements beyond making those standalone delivery options explicit.
implementation: Updated docs/roadmap.html Milestone 30 planning notes to call out the portable static-bundle delivery strategy.
Added a Standalone Delivery Modes capability card to docs/roadmap.html.
Mirrored the same delivery-mode planning into docs/llm-project-context.json so future milestone reviews see it up front.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by adding bundled local-launch groundwork to standalone exports.
decisions: The shipped game should remain one static web bundle even when launcher conveniences are added.
A bundled local launcher should be treated as a delivery helper over the exported bundle, not as a second runtime model.
Windows-first helper scripts are a useful first step because they prove the packaging seam without forcing a full desktop-wrapper commitment.
implementation: Extended StandaloneDistributionManifest with launcher metadata such as script paths, default port, and launcher notes.
Updated apps/api/src/standalone-bundle.ts to emit launch/run-local.ps1 and launch/run-local.cmd into the standalone bundle.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts so standalone preview and release readiness report bundled local-launch availability.
Extended publishing and standalone-bundle unit tests to verify launcher metadata and bundled launcher files.

entries.summary: Turned the remaining editor, player-front-end, renderer-choice, and starter-library work into a durable milestone-mapped checklist.
decisions: The unfinished work should be tracked explicitly as a remaining-feature checklist instead of being inferred from older milestone notes.
Future milestone starts should always name which remaining checklist items the milestone is meant to move.
Renderer-family choice belongs in future planning as a first-class designer decision once multiple presentation families exist.
implementation: Added a Remaining Feature Checklist section to docs/roadmap.html with milestone-mapped work for Milestones 36 through 39.
Updated docs/llm-project-context.json with the same checklist and a standing rule to summarize relevant remaining items at the start of each future milestone.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by packaging player-facing handoff instructions into standalone exports.
decisions: A standalone export should explain to the recipient how to run it, not just carry the correct files.
Launch and hosting guidance should travel with the package as bundle files and manifest metadata rather than living only in editor UI copy.
The editor should surface packaged handoff-guide presence in preview and release readiness so designers can verify the player-facing instructions before export.
implementation: Extended StandaloneDistributionManifest with handoff metadata for README file paths, recommended launch path, and delivery modes.
Updated apps/api/src/standalone-bundle.ts to emit README.html and README.txt into the standalone bundle.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts so standalone preview and release readiness report packaged handoff guides and recommended launch guidance.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and tests/unit/standalone-bundle.test.mjs to verify the new metadata and bundled instruction files.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, and LLM-readable context for the new slice.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by making the editable forkable export path previewable inside the editor.
decisions: The two publishing modes should both be inspectable in the editor so the difference between editable and play-only handoffs is explicit.
Forkable preview state should invalidate when the draft changes, just like standalone preview state, so the editor does not show stale artifact summaries.
Release readiness should represent both export modes instead of focusing only on the standalone package.
implementation: Added Preview Forkable Artifact and a dedicated forkable preview card in apps/web/editor.html.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts to fetch the latest release-backed forkableProject artifact, render its editable-handoff details, and include forkable preview status in Release Readiness.
Updated tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1 so the forkable preview button, disabled state, and preview panel are covered by the real browser smoke test.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, and LLM-readable context for the new slice.

entries.summary: Corrected the roadmap milestone-status view so completed milestones and active milestone slices match the actual repo history.
decisions: Milestones 15 through 28 should visually read as completed just like earlier complete milestones.
Milestone 29 should be shown as complete because slices 29A through 29E are finished.
Milestone 30 should be shown as active and complete through 30K rather than still labeled planned.
implementation: Updated docs/roadmap.html so status-done milestones share complete styling, added a Milestone Status Snapshot section, marked Milestone 29 complete, and marked Milestone 30 active through 30K.
Updated docs/llm-project-context.json with a machine-readable currentMilestoneStatus block so future milestone reviews have a durable source of truth.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by making forkable exports self-describing instead of leaving editable handoff guidance only in editor prose.
decisions: Forkable exports should carry release-backed import guidance and known limitations just like standalone exports carry distribution and handoff metadata.
The immutable release should remain the source of truth for release label, version, id, and notes in forkable exports.
Release Readiness should summarize both the existence of a forkable preview and the presence of forkable handoff guidance.
implementation: Added ForkableProjectManifest to packages/publishing and attached it to forkableProject artifacts with release metadata, content counts, import guidance, handoff next steps, and known limitations.
Updated apps/api/src/index.ts so forkable artifact generation receives immutable release metadata.

Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts so the forkable preview and release-readiness views surface the manifest-backed recommended file name, import area, next steps, release notes summary, and known limitations.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and chat log.

entries.summary: Added the future guided forkable-import vision to the roadmap and machine-readable project context.
decisions: Forkable distributions should import as new editable projects, not overwrite the source release.
The future import flow should validate the artifact, show provenance and release metadata, preview starter packs and custom libraries, and later support selective remix import.
This belongs with editor-completion work because the export side now exists and the missing work is the editor-side import/fork UX.
implementation: Updated docs/roadmap.html to add guided forkable-project import under the Milestone 36 editor-completion checklist and to add a Forkable Project Import capability card.
Updated docs/llm-project-context.json to mirror the same future publishing and editor-completion planning.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by adding a side-by-side comparison panel for forkable and standalone release artifacts.
decisions: Designers should be able to compare editable and play-only release handoffs in one place rather than mentally merging two separate preview cards.
The comparison panel should stay useful even before a release exists by explaining the default difference between the two artifact types.
This comparison view complements the future forkable import workflow by clarifying which export is meant for remixing versus player distribution.
implementation: Added an Artifact Comparison card to apps/web/editor.html.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts to render forkable-versus-standalone purpose, handoff, and shared-release comparison lines from the current preview state.
Updated tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1 to verify the comparison panel renders helpful status text and list content.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and chat log.

entries.summary: Reviewed application QA notes and WorldTree application notes, then folded the resulting fixes and future implementation order into the remaining-feature milestone plan.
decisions: Editor completion should start with a project-first Adventure Hub instead of sample-first boot so users are not confused about whether they are editing the sample adventure or creating a new one.
The WorldTree metaphor should shape top-level editor information architecture and onboarding language, but object-level labels should stay practical and explicit.
Validation should be routed contextually to the workspace where the issue can be fixed, rather than dumping all warnings into the Adventure screen.
The legacy ACS timed-action mode should return as a supported player-front-end mode with movement-bar drain on move, idle trickle, and pause only through the player/toolbox panel.
Starter-library completion should explicitly include legal-safe renaming or replacement of risky names plus western, mythology, medieval, and steampunk genre packs.
Every future milestone completion continues to require the full end-to-end quality gates through npm run quality and npm test.
implementation: Updated docs/roadmap.html Remaining Feature Checklist so Milestone 36 now covers Adventure Hub, WorldTree-shaped editor flow, help-icon tooltips, contextual validation, World Atlas cleanup, and terminology cleanup.
Updated docs/roadmap.html so Milestone 37 now covers timed-action mode, richer player profile/state surfaces, and dialogue/input readiness for future AI conversations.
Updated docs/roadmap.html so Milestone 38 now explicitly includes presentation-family choice at new-adventure creation time.
Updated docs/roadmap.html so Milestone 39 now includes legal-safe starter-pack naming plus western, mythology, medieval, and steampunk starter-pack expansion.
Mirrored the same milestone-mapped planning into docs/llm-project-context.json so future milestone reviews see the new durable checklist.

entries.summary: Added steampunk to the planned future starter-pack genre expansion.
decisions: Steampunk should be tracked as a first-class future starter-pack genre alongside western, mythology, and medieval.
The durable roadmap and AI-readable project context should list steampunk anywhere the planned starter-pack expansion appears.
implementation: Updated docs/roadmap.html Milestone 39 starter-pack expansion language to include steampunk.
Updated docs/llm-project-context.json remainingFeatureChecklist milestone39StarterLibrariesAndGraphics entry to include steampunk.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by aligning export filenames and packaged release-note files with the publishing manifests.
decisions: Final handoff names should come from the publishing manifests rather than editor-only fallback naming rules.
Standalone bundles should include a plain-text release-notes file so recipients receive the same release context reflected in the editor preview.
Preview, readiness, and comparison panels should show the final exported handoff names so distribution review matches the actual download.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts so the standalone handoff manifest now carries recommended archive name, extracted-folder name, and release-notes file path metadata.
Updated apps/api/src/standalone-bundle.ts to emit RELEASE-NOTES.txt and mention it inside the packaged README files.

Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts so forkable downloads use the manifest-backed recommended JSON file name and standalone downloads use the manifest-backed ZIP file name, with preview/readiness/comparison surfaces showing the final handoff names.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and tests/unit/standalone-bundle.test.mjs, then updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, and LLM-readable context.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by packaging the editable forkable handoff as a ZIP archive with companion guidance files and release notes.
decisions: The editable handoff should be just as self-explanatory and shareable as the standalone handoff instead of remaining a raw JSON download.
The forkable artifact itself should remain structured JSON inside the package so future import flows can still target that canonical payload.
Forkable preview, readiness, and comparison panels should surface the final package names and packaged companion files before export.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts so ForkableProjectManifest.handoff now carries recommended archive name, extracted-folder name, packaged artifact filename, README paths, and a release-notes path.
Added packages/publishing/src/forkable-package.ts plus shared text-file ZIP support in packages/publishing/src/standalone-archive.ts so forkable exports can be packaged into a ZIP archive.

Updated apps/web/editor.html and apps/web/src/editor.ts so the editor exports a Forkable Package ZIP, downloads it with manifest-backed names, and shows the packaged file details in Forkable Artifact Preview, Release Readiness, and Artifact Comparison.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, and LLM-readable context.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by making the forkable package itself a typed manifest-backed artifact instead of only a ZIP assembled at download time.
decisions: The editable handoff should mirror the standalone handoff by carrying an explicit package manifest with file list and entry-file data.
Forkable ZIP creation, preview, and validation should all read from that one shared package description instead of re-deriving package contents in separate places.
The editor should show packaged file count and file paths for the forkable handoff before export.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts with forkable package manifest/file types and attached the package manifest during createForkableProjectExport(...).
Updated packages/publishing/src/forkable-package.ts to build the ZIP from the shared package manifest.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts to show forkable package entry file, packaged file count, and packaged file paths in preview/readiness/comparison surfaces.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and updated roadmap, version history, LLM-readable context, and chat log.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by giving the entire release one typed handoff summary that can be previewed in the editor and shipped inside both export modes.
decisions: Designers should not have to reconstruct the release story from separate forkable and standalone preview panels.
Both artifact types should carry the same release-level handoff manifest so the editor and packaged outputs tell the same editable-versus-play-only story.
The shared release handoff summary should be previewable directly from Test and Publish and should be packaged as RELEASE-HANDOFF.json.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts with ReleaseHandoffManifest and attached it to both forkableProject and standalonePlayable artifacts.
Updated packages/publishing/src/index.ts and apps/api/src/standalone-bundle.ts so both package types now include RELEASE-HANDOFF.json alongside their existing packaged guidance files.
Updated apps/web/editor.html and apps/web/src/editor.ts to add Preview Release Handoff and a dedicated Release Handoff Manifest card in Test and Publish.

Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs, tests/unit/standalone-bundle.test.mjs, and tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1, then updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, and LLM-readable context.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by turning the shared release handoff manifest into a first-class downloadable release document in the editor.
decisions: The shared release handoff manifest should not only be previewable or packaged indirectly inside export bundles; it should also be exportable directly on its own.
The direct export should use a manifest-backed recommended filename rather than editor-only fallback naming.
The new export control should live beside the existing release handoff preview and package export actions in Test and Publish.
implementation: Updated apps/web/editor.html and apps/web/src/editor.ts to add Export Release Handoff, keep the shared manifest current, and download it directly as JSON.
Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts so ReleaseHandoffManifest carries a recommended direct-export filename.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1, then updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, and LLM-readable context.

entries.summary: Promoted PDF regeneration and screenshot currency into a standing milestone-closeout requirement.
decisions: Human-facing PDF documents must always be regenerated as part of every milestone build, not treated as optional after-the-fact cleanup.
Guide and reference screenshots should always be current and reflect the exact task or purpose they illustrate.
User Guide tutorial screenshots should show each step explicitly rather than relying on repeated or generic images.
implementation: Updated docs/roadmap.html milestone-closeout language to require regenerated PDFs and refreshed screenshots.
Updated docs/testing-strategy.md to treat PDF regeneration and screenshot refresh as part of milestone acceptance.
Updated docs/llm-project-context.json so future milestone work reads the same documentation-quality requirements from durable project memory.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by adding a shared artifact-integrity report that compares the forkable package, standalone bundle, and shared release handoff summary before distribution.
decisions: Milestone 30 should add one more packaging-confidence checkpoint before moving on to later editor and player milestones.
The integrity report should be built in the pure publishing layer so the editor can preview and export it without inventing ad hoc browser-only rules.
The report should focus on package parity: release metadata, shared handoff parity, required packaged files, archive names, and release-notes paths.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts with ArtifactIntegrityReport and createArtifactIntegrityReport(...).

Updated apps/web/editor.html and apps/web/src/editor.ts to add Preview Artifact Integrity, Export Integrity Report, a dedicated Artifact Integrity Report card, and readiness messaging in Test and Publish.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1, then updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, and LLM-readable context.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by bundling the shared release handoff summary and artifact-integrity report into one reviewer-facing ZIP package.
decisions: The next Milestone 30 slice should stay on the publishing closure track and produce one reviewer-facing deliverable instead of jumping ahead to later editor or player milestones.
The release review package should be built in the publishing layer as a typed manifest plus ZIP archive, not assembled ad hoc in the browser.
The package should include README files, release notes, the shared handoff JSON, and the artifact-integrity JSON.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts with ReleaseReviewPackageManifest and createReleaseReviewPackageManifest(...), and added packages/publishing/src/review-package.ts for ZIP creation.

Updated apps/web/editor.html and apps/web/src/editor.ts to add Preview Review Package, Export Review Package, a dedicated Release Review Package card, and release-readiness messaging in Test and Publish.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs and tools/editor-ui-smoke.ps1, then updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 30 by making the reviewer-facing ZIP self-describing with its own packaged manifest file.
decisions: The Release Review Package should follow the same manifest-backed pattern as the forkable package, standalone package, and shared release handoff summary.
The reviewer ZIP should include a packaged review-package-manifest.json file instead of relying only on preview text and the ZIP builder.
The editor should surface the direct review-manifest filename and packaged manifest file in preview and readiness text.
implementation: Extended packages/publishing/src/index.ts so ReleaseReviewPackageManifest carries recommended and packaged manifest filenames and now includes review-package-manifest.json inside the review ZIP.
Updated apps/web/src/editor.ts to show the direct review-manifest filename and packaged manifest file in Release Review Package preview and readiness text.
Extended tests/unit/publishing.test.mjs, then updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs.

entries.summary: Started Milestone 31 by creating a pure AI-provider contract layer instead of wiring any specific LLM directly into the editor or runtime.
decisions: Milestone 31 should begin with a package-level provider boundary so later OpenAI, Anthropic, local-model, or agent adapters can all target the same structured contract.
The first slice should stay UI-free and network-free: provider manifests, generation requests, proposal envelopes, provider registries, and validation helpers.
AI-generated content must remain reviewable structured data; no later milestone should let a provider mutate adventure data without going through validation and explicit editor acceptance.
implementation: Added packages/ai-core with AiProviderManifest, AdventureGenerationRequest, AiAdventureProposal, createAiProviderRegistry(...), findAiProvider(...), listProvidersForCapability(...), validateAdventureGenerationRequest(...), and validateAdventureProposal(...).
Updated tsconfig.base.json and tsconfig.json so the new workspace package participates in builds and path resolution.
Added tests/unit/ai-core.test.mjs, then updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 31 by adding provider-independent generation plans and proposal review reports to the pure AI contract layer.
decisions: The next AI-foundation slice should stay package-level and avoid editor/API wiring until the review boundary is durable.
Future AI UI needs one standard generation-plan sequence so different providers do not create inconsistent human-review flows.
Proposal application readiness should depend on one shared review report that combines request validation, proposal validation, provider warnings, and explicit acceptance state.
implementation: Extended packages/ai-core/src/index.ts with AdventureGenerationPlan, AiProposalReviewReport, createAdventureGenerationPlan(...), and createProposalReviewReport(...).
Extended tests/unit/ai-core.test.mjs to cover human-review-first plan creation, blocked review reports, and accepted ready-to-apply review reports.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs for Milestone 31B.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 31 by adding portable generation session records to the AI contract layer.
decisions: The next AI-foundation slice should stay package-level and make the review workflow portable before any editor/API persistence UI is added.
Later AI-assisted authoring needs one stable handoff object that keeps request, plan, proposal, and review data linked together instead of spreading that linkage across browser-only state.
Session validation should catch mismatched provider ids, request ids, proposal ids, readiness state, and apply eligibility before later persistence or import/export layers rely on the session.
implementation: Extended packages/ai-core/src/index.ts with AiGenerationSessionRecord, createGenerationSessionRecord(...), and validateGenerationSessionRecord(...).
Extended tests/unit/ai-core.test.mjs to cover valid session creation and mismatched session linkage validation.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs for Milestone 31C.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 31 by adding proposal impact summaries to the AI contract layer.
decisions: The next AI-foundation slice should stay package-level and make proposal impact easier to inspect before any UI acceptance flow is built.
Later AI review screens should not need to diff whole AdventurePackage payloads just to answer what the proposal changes.
Patch-only proposals that lack a structured adventure payload should report that honestly instead of pretending they have object-count deltas.
implementation: Extended packages/ai-core/src/index.ts with AiProposalChangeSummary and createProposalChangeSummary(...).
Extended tests/unit/ai-core.test.mjs to cover change-summary deltas against an existing adventure baseline and the no-structured-payload fallback message.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs for Milestone 31D.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 31 by adding safe proposal application plans to the AI contract layer.
decisions: The next AI-foundation slice should answer the final review-phase question before UI wiring: can this proposal be applied yet, and what sections would it touch?
Application planning should be derived from the existing review report and change summary instead of inventing a second set of browser-only business rules.
Blocked or patch-only proposals should expose explicit blockers rather than leaving apply state ambiguous.
implementation: Extended packages/ai-core/src/index.ts with AiProposalApplicationPlan and createProposalApplicationPlan(...).
Extended tests/unit/ai-core.test.mjs to cover accepted structured application plans and blocked plans when review or payload state is incomplete.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs for Milestone 31E.

entries.summary: Continued Milestone 31 by adding portable AI review packages to the AI contract layer.
decisions: The next AI-foundation slice should stay package-level and bundle the review artifacts into one portable handoff object before any editor or API export UI is added.
Later AI-assisted authoring flows should not have to reconstruct package filenames, required files, or README guidance from browser-only state.
Package validation should catch mismatched session, request, proposal, readiness, and required-file state before later persistence or import/export layers rely on the package.
implementation: Extended packages/ai-core/src/index.ts with AiGenerationSessionPackage, AiGenerationSessionPackageManifest, createGenerationSessionPackage(...), and validateGenerationSessionPackage(...).
Extended tests/unit/ai-core.test.mjs to cover valid portable AI review package creation plus mismatched linkage, missing required files, and missing README text.
Updated roadmap, version history, System Reference, User Guide, LLM-readable context, and regenerated PDFs for Milestone 31F.
